description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both featuring a dark, weathered metallic finish. The blades are joined at a central, ornate metallic hub with gold-toned decorative elements, giving the axe a medieval or fantasy appearance. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood, reinforced with metal bands and a textured grip section near the base for secure handling. The bottom of the handle is capped with a metal ring, possibly for hanging or attaching a lanyard. The overall construction suggests durability and a design intended for both display and functional use. The axe's imposing size and detailed craftsmanship make it suitable for urban settings such as themed parks, historical reenactments, or as a decorative piece in garages or workshops.
description: A double-headed battle axe with weathered metal blades, ornate gold-toned hub, and a long wooden handle with a textured grip and metal ring at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades, ornate central hub, and full handle with visible grip and metal ring.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying both blades, central hub, and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with metal bands, textured leather grip, and a metal ring at the base.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, wide blades with a dark, weathered finish and a central ornate gold-toned hub; no beard, with a broad cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible metal reinforcement at the hub.
